zh.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth於1250年出生在英國英格蘭爾沃斯。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1318年在他的家鄉享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth于1250年出生在英国英格兰爾沃斯。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1318年在他的家乡享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Worth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Worth, for example in Cheshire, Dorset, Sussex, and Kent, from Old English worth ‘enclosure, settlement’. The vocabulary word probably survived into the Middle English period in the sense of a subsidiary settlement dependent on a main village, and in some cases the surname may be a topographic name derived from this use. Compare Worthy .
North German: topographic name from Middle Low German wort ‘enclosure, elevated piece of land, farmstead’, or a habitational name from a place so named in northern Germany.

variants.name: Word
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of Dutch Van der Woord, a habitational name from any of the places called Woord or Woerd.

variants.name: Wortmann
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone who lived on a raised, secure site, Middle Low German wort + man ‘man’. Compare Worth 2.

variants.name: Worthy
meanings: English: from Middle English worthi ‘enclosure’ (Old English worthig, weorthig, wurthig). The surname is probably habitational, from a place so named, such as Worthy (Hampshire, Devon) and Worthy Hill Farm (Wiltshire).

variants.name: Wurdeman
meanings: Americanized form of North German Wurdemann, also Würdemann: topographic name from Middle Low German wurt ‘farm area, field, woodland’. Compare Worth .
Americanized form of German Würdemann: from an ancient Germanic personal name formed with Old High German wirdi ‘dignity’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Wirth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Gunter, Dieter, Ernst, Gerhard, Manfred, Reinhold, Armin, Erna.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for an inn-keeper, Middle High German wirt, German Wirt.
German: status name for a man who was head of a family and master of his own household, from the same word in the sense ‘provider’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth was born in 1250, in Worth, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1318, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth est né en 1250, à Worth, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1318, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth wurde 1250 in Worth, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1318 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas de Worth è nato il nel 1250 nel Worth, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1318, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni.
